## Release Notes (each ha-frontend release in this pin bump)

### 20260624.5 — 2026-07-10T12:59:12Z (stable)

- Fix focus loss in text selector with multiple (@MindFreeze - #52980)
- Match Statistic card line height to Entity card (@MindFreeze - #52981)
- Add entity update tracking again in Automation and Script editor (@silamon - #53002)
- No need to show rename when alias is already provided (@silamon - #53003)
- Fix history/logbook entity selection leaking across browser tabs (@MindFreeze - #53014)
- Fix My redirect for AI tasks to /config/ai-tasks (@MindFreeze - #53015)
- Fix tile trend graph rendering blank when shown by a visibility condition (@MindFreeze - #53016)
- Fix number/input_number slider clipped in entities card at certain zooms (@MindFreeze - #53017)
- Fix escape key discarding unsaved changes in dashboard editor (@MindFreeze - #53029)
- Only show solar/battery legend in energy usage graph when configured (@MindFreeze - #53030)
- Fix target picker "Add target" popover opening off to the right (@MindFreeze - #53041)
- Fix cloning when drag-sorting a section to the last position (@MindFreeze - #53050)
- Wrap device automation picker option labels instead of truncating (@MindFreeze - #53065)
- Restore per-second updating of ha-relative-time (@MindFreeze - #53070)

---

## HIGH IMPACT

No high-impact breaking changes are present in this release. No CSS custom properties were renamed or removed, no web component element names were changed, no shadow DOM structure was restructured, and no slots or parts were removed that the ha-lcars theme targets.

---

## MEDIUM IMPACT

### Component: `hui-statistic-card` (`.name` line-height and `.info` layout)

**What changed:**
In `src/panels/lovelace/cards/hui-statistic-card.ts`, two CSS changes were made inside the component's shadow DOM:

1. The `.name` element's `line-height` was changed from `var(--ha-line-height-expanded)` to the hard-coded value `40px`.
2. The `.info` block gained `display: flex`, `align-items: baseline`, and `line-height: var(--ha-line-height-condensed)`. A new child selector `.info > *` now owns `overflow: hidden`, `white-space: nowrap`, and `text-overflow: ellipsis` (previously on `.info` itself).

**What it affects in ha-lcars:**
The ha-lcars theme sets `secondary-text-color: var(--lcars-primary-text)` globally, which the statistic card's `.name` element uses for its color. The line-height change from `var(--ha-line-height-expanded)` to the hard-coded `40px` means the `.name` row is now explicitly `40px` tall — this matches the LCARS `--header-height: 40px` and is likely harmless, but the theme can no longer override this via `--ha-line-height-expanded` if it ever wished to. More relevantly, the `.info` block is now a flex row with `align-items: baseline` and its text-clipping properties have moved from `.info` to `.info > *`. If any card-mod rule in ha-lcars ever injected CSS into `hui-statistic-card` targeting `.info` for overflow/truncation control, those rules would now need to target `.info > *` instead.

**Current ha-lcars state:**
The theme does not inject card-mod CSS specifically into `hui-statistic-card`'s shadow DOM — it relies on global `card-mod-card-yaml` (`.:`) rules which operate on the `:host` and `ha-card` level, not inside the shadow DOM of the card's internal elements. However, `secondary-text-color` flows into `.name`'s color, and the new `.info` flex layout may interact with text-length assumptions if the LCARS font (Tungsten/Antonio, which is wide and uppercase) causes text in `.info > *` to behave differently under the new flex+`white-space: nowrap` model.

**Suggested fix:**
No immediate fix required. Monitor whether LCARS-fonted statistic cards display `.info` content (secondary stat value and unit) correctly under the new flex layout. If truncation or overflow issues appear, add a targeted card-mod rule:

```yaml
"hui-statistic-card$": |
  .info > * {
    overflow: hidden;
    white-space: nowrap;
    text-overflow: ellipsis;
  }
```

---

### Component: `hui-input-number-entity-row` and `hui-number-entity-row` (slider margin)

**What changed:**
In both `hui-input-number-entity-row.ts` and `hui-number-entity-row.ts`, the `ha-slider` element inside the entity row gained:
```css
margin: 1px var(--ha-space-2);
```
This adds a horizontal margin (keyed to `--ha-space-2`) and a 1 px vertical margin to the slider thumb area, preventing clipping at the card's `overflow-x: hidden` boundary.

**What it affects in ha-lcars:**
The ha-lcars theme applies extensive entity-row styling via `card-mod-row-yaml`. Specifically, the `div.flex` selector is targeted:
```yaml
div.flex {
  flex-grow: 0;
}
```
The `div.flex` wraps the slider in these rows. The new `margin: 1px var(--ha-space-2)` on `ha-slider` inside that `div.flex` could interact with the LCARS `--lcars-height: 60px` row height. The 1 px top/bottom margin is negligible, but the horizontal margin of `--ha-space-2` (typically 8 px in HA's spacing scale) will visually indent the slider from the row edges — this may look slightly off within LCARS entity-styled rows where the readout block is sized precisely to `var(--lcars-inner-readout-width)`.

Additionally, `max-width: 200px` remains on `ha-slider`, so LCARS entity rows with the `entity-lozenge-*` / `entity-barrel-*` / etc. classes that use fixed `--lcars-inner-readout-width` should remain correctly sized, but the horizontal inset from `--ha-space-2` may cause a small visual gap.

**Suggested fix:**
If the horizontal gap looks wrong in LCARS-styled slider rows, override the slider margin within the entity row card-mod. Add to `card-mod-row-yaml` under the `"hui-generic-entity-row $"` block:

```css
@container style(--lcars-entity-class) {
  ha-slider {
    margin-inline: 0;
  }
}
```

---

## LOW IMPACT

### Component: `hui-trend-graph-card-feature` (ResizeController + state history caching)

**What changed:**
The trend graph card feature gained a `ResizeController` from `@lit-labs/observers/resize-controller` and an internal `_stateHistory` cache. This means the component now re-renders when its container is resized and no longer blanks when shown via a visibility condition. All changes are internal rendering/data logic with no new CSS custom properties, element names, or shadow DOM structural changes.

**What it affects in ha-lcars:**
No LCARS theme CSS targets `hui-trend-graph-card-feature` by name. This is a pure behavior fix.

**Suggested fix:**
No fix needed. This is informational — the trend graph will now render correctly when revealed by a visibility condition, which is a visual improvement that benefits the theme without requiring any theme changes.

---

### Component: `hui-energy-usage-graph-card` (conditional solar/battery legend)

**What changed:**
The `used_solar` and `used_battery` series entries in `combinedData` are now only added when the corresponding source (`statIdsByCat.solar` / `statIdsByCat.from_battery`) is actually configured. Previously, empty legend entries appeared for grid-only setups.

**What it affects in ha-lcars:**
No LCARS theme CSS targets the energy usage graph's legend elements or its data series colors beyond the global `energy-non-fossil-color: var(--lcars-alt-green)` variable in `defaults.yaml`, which is unaffected. This is a data/rendering fix only.

**Suggested fix:**
No fix needed.

---

### Component: `hui-sections-view` (`.rollback` property removed from sortable)

**What changed:**
The `.rollback=${false}` property binding was removed from the `ha-sortable` element in `hui-sections-view.ts`. This is an internal drag-sort behavior change (prevents rollback animation when dragging a section to the last position).

**What it affects in ha-lcars:**
No LCARS theme CSS or card-mod rule targets the `.rollback` attribute or any drag-state class in `hui-sections-view`. The sortable ghost/drag states in `ha-config-automation` and `ha-config-script` are themed (`.sortable-ghost`, `.sortable-drag` → `background-color: var(--lcars-ui-secondary)`), but those are in the automation/script editors, not in the sections view.

**Suggested fix:**
No fix needed.
